summary: Comprehensive backend + frontend testing of Branch Transfer system. All 22 backend tests pass. UI mostly functional with 1 critical bug found (initial ephemeral row pattern prevents product search from working until 'Add Product' is clicked). Key flows validated: markup template, product lookup, transfer_capital auto-fill, price memory, validation rules, override checkbox, order creation, send, receive dialog. Receive fails with 'Insufficient stock' when 'All Branches' is selected (from_branch_id empty) - expected UX issue, works correctly when Main Branch is selected.

minor.issue: Returns 400 'Insufficient stock: have 0, need N' when order created with empty from_branch_id (All Branches selected). Expected from context but affects real usage.

ui_bugs.component: BranchTransferPage - New Transfer - Product Search Row
ui_bugs.issue: CRITICAL BUG: Initial row is ephemeral (not in React state). rows is initialized as [] in useState, rendering uses [newRow()] which is NOT stored in state. Typing in the initial search field calls updateRow(ephemeralId) which fails silently (ID not in rows array). Users MUST click 'Add Product' first to get a real row before searching. This is a confusing UX flow.
ui_bugs.rca: In BranchTransferPage.js: useState([]) initializes rows as empty. JSX renders {(rows.length ? rows : [newRow()]).map(row => ...)}. The ephemeral newRow() is not in state, so onChange→searchProduct→updateRow finds no matching row ID and does nothing. Fix: Change useState([]) to useState([newRow()]) or useState(() => [newRow()]) to initialize with one real row.

ui_bugs.issue: View Details, Mark as Sent, Cancel buttons for draft/sent orders are missing data-testid attributes. Only receive-btn-{id} has a testid. Send and Cancel buttons use title='Mark as Sent' and title='Cancel' for identification.
ui_bugs.selector: button[title='Mark as Sent'], button[title='Cancel'], button[title='View Details']

integration_issues.issue: When toBranchId is selected and rows=[], the rendered ephemeral row cannot accept input. User must click 'Add Product' button first to create a real row in state before product search works.
Creating transfer with 'All Branches' selected (from_branch_id='') makes receive fail: 'Insufficient stock: have 0, need N'. Must select specific Main Branch before creating transfers.

issues: React hydration errors: 'In HTML, span cannot be a child of tbody/tr'. Live preview tool (x-dynamic attributes) injects span wrappers inside table elements, causing these hydration warnings. Not a production issue.

action_items: CRITICAL FIX: In BranchTransferPage.js, change `useState([])` to `useState(() => [newRow()])` so the initial row is always a real row in state. This fixes the ephemeral row bug where product search doesn't work without clicking 'Add Product' first.

MINOR: Add data-testid attributes to View Details, Mark as Sent, and Cancel buttons in history table for better testability: data-testid='view-btn-{id}', data-testid='send-btn-{id}', data-testid='cancel-btn-{id}'
UX: Consider showing warning or auto-selecting Main Branch when user navigates to Branch Transfers with 'All Branches' selected, since from_branch_id is required for correct receive workflow
critical_code_review_comments: BranchTransferPage.js: `useState([])` + `{(rows.length ? rows : [newRow()]).map(...)}` pattern creates ephemeral non-state rows that cannot accept user interaction. Fix: `useState(() => [newRow()])`
History buttons: Only receive-btn has data-testid. View Details, Mark as Sent, Cancel buttons lack testids making them harder to test/automate
Receive endpoint: When fromBranchId is '' (empty), `find_one({'branch_id': ''})` returns null → 400 error. The page should enforce branch selection before allowing transfers.

success_rate.frontend: 80% (critical flows work after workaround of clicking Add Product first; validation, override, send, history all work)

seed_data_creation: Created 5 transfer orders (BTO-0001 to BTO-0005) via backend tests and UI. BTO-0003 and BTO-0005 were received. Backend test data cleaned up (orders cancelled or received). Price memory set: ENERTONE last_retail=1020, last_transfer_capital=970 for IPIL BRANCH.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Main bug: BranchTransferPage.js has ephemeral initial row (useState([]) issue). Fix is to change to useState(() => [newRow()]). After fix, test: (1) product search works immediately on page load without clicking Add Product, (2) transfer_capital auto-fills to 970 for ENERTONE/Feeds/+₱50, (3) branch_retail auto-fills 1020 from price memory. Backend tests all pass (22/22). Use 'Add Product' button first as workaround in UI tests. Full receive flow requires selecting Main Branch (not All Branches) before creating a transfer.
rca_of_issue: Root cause: In BranchTransferPage.js, `const [rows, setRows] = useState([])` initializes rows as empty array. The JSX renders `{(rows.length ? rows : [newRow()]).map(row => ...)}` which creates a temporary row via newRow() not stored in state. When user types in the search field, onChange calls `searchProduct(row.id, query)` → `updateRow(row.id, {productSearch: query})` → `setRows(prev => prev.map(r => r.id === id ? {...r, ...updates} : r))`. Since the ephemeral row.id is not in the prev array (empty), map returns [], state doesn't update, component re-renders with empty rows, showing the ephemeral placeholder again. Fix: Initialize with `useState(() => [newRow()])` so rows always has at least one real row.
